race.name: Gran Fondo Utah
race.tagline: Ride the Wasatch Front: Conquer Nebo Loop in Utah's Gran Fondo National Series

vitals.location: Payson Memorial Park, 300 S Main St, Payson, UT 84651, USA

climate.overview: Early-mid June in Payson (Wasatch Front, ~4,500 ft): mild/warm daytime highs 70-85°F, possible afternoon thunderstorms at altitude.

terrain.overview: Road cycling through Wasatch Front: iconic Nebo Loop climb (>9,000 ft), Goshen Canyon, fruit orchards, Utah Lake.

logistics.explanation: Easy SLC airport access, local bike shops, central park start/finish, app-supported.

length.explanation: Gran at 93mi/150km with three timed segments offers epic distance.

technicality.explanation: Pure road; no gravel or tech descents, just climbs.

elevation.explanation: 6,800ft on Gran with Nebo Loop punch.

climate.explanation: June warmth ideal, but altitude storms a wildcard.

adventure.explanation: Tour of Utah climbs, canyons, orchards: Utah scenery maxed.

prestige.explanation: National Series points, Nationals qualifier; growing rep.

race_quality.explanation: Timed segments, teams, custom kits, solid support.

experience.explanation: Festival, food trucks, app results, podiums.

community.explanation: All levels welcome, age groups, teams.

field_depth.explanation: New event; open field, no elite depth yet.

expenses.explanation: Travel to Utah adds up; reg fair.

biased_opinion.overview: New star in Gran Fondo National Series shines with Nebo Loop's Tour of Utah legacy, timed segments, and Rocky Mountain qualifier status. Gran route's 93mi/6,800ft delivers suffering and views; perfect for mid-pack hammers chasing series points. Logistics smooth, vibe fun—don't sleep on this.

final_verdict.one_liner: Epic Wasatch climbs meet National Series prestige: must-do for road warriors.
final_verdict.full_verdict: Gran Fondo Utah nails the gran fondo formula: challenging Nebo Loop centerpiece, multiple distances, timed racing without mass-start chaos, plus qualifiers and kits. 2nd edition builds buzz; ideal summer road event despite youth.

logistics.overview: Start/finish Payson Memorial Park; packet pickup Thu-Sat; app for maps/results; BikeReg registration; SLC airport ~60mi; local shops Spanish Fork/Payson.
logistics.registration_notes: Gran/Medio $150 (early)-$200 (onsite); Piccolo $150; via BikeReg; teams available.

history.overview: Founded 2025 as inaugural event by Gran Fondo National Series to expand in Rockies; 2026 is 2nd annual.

course_description.character: Timed-segment road race through Wasatch Front beauty: brutal Nebo Loop climb, scenic canyons/orchards/lake.

suffering_zones.label: Nebo Loop (>9,000 ft, Tour of Utah KOM)

citations.title: Gran Fondo Utah Online Registration - BikeReg.com

research_metadata.notes: Event new (2025 inaugural); distances/elev from primary site; GPS approx from address; no past results/notable moments; climate inferred from location/season.

youtube_data.search_query: Gran Fondo Utah gran fondo cycling Payson Memorial Park

videos.title: Stunning Road Trip: St. George to Salt Lake City, Utah - Scenic Driving Tour - 4K
videos.description:
Enjoy this 4K scenic driving tour from St. George to Salt Lake City, Utah, on I-15 North. On this road trip, you'll pass through Hurricane, Cedar City, Beaver, Provo, and finally reach Salt Lake City. You'll see beautiful desert landscapes, stunning mountain views, and the open roads of Utah.
➤ SUBSCRIBE for more scenic drives ➤ http://subscribe.worldofdrives.com/

Perfect for anyone who loves a scenic drive and travel adventure, this video captures the best of Utah’s nature and highways. Watch the journey from St. George to Salt Lake City on I-15 and get inspired for your next travel adventure. Enjoy this incredible driving experience in Utah right from your screen. This scenic drive is shot in 4K.

Salt Lake City
alt Lake City, the capital and most populous city of Utah, is nestled in the northern part of the state and serves as the cultural and economic heart of the Salt Lake Valley. Established in 1847 by Mormon pioneers led by Brigham Young, the city is renowned for its historical and religious significance as the headquarters of the Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints. With a backdrop of the stunning Wasatch Range to the east and the Great Salt Lake to the northwest, Salt Lake City offers a unique blend of urban amenities and outdoor recreational opportunities. It is famous for hosting the 2002 Winter Olympics and is a gateway to world-class skiing, hiking, and other outdoor activities. The city's vibrant downtown features the Utah State Capitol, numerous cultural attractions, and a thriving arts scene, making it a dynamic and diverse metropolis.

St George
St. George is a vibrant city located in the southwestern corner of Utah, renowned for its stunning red rock landscapes and mild climate. Founded in 1861 by Mormon settlers, it has grown into a key hub for tourism and outdoor recreation, particularly as a gateway to Zion National Park and other natural attractions. The city is a popular destination for those seeking a mix of outdoor activities, including hiking, biking, and golfing, thanks to its scenic surroundings and ample sunshine. St. George is also known for its cultural offerings, with a variety of festivals, arts events, and historic sites that reflect its rich pioneer heritage. Its blend of natural beauty, recreational opportunities, and cultural attractions make St. George a dynamic and appealing place to live and visit.
